BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Moist and wet avalanches are the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The Avalanche Warning Service currently has only a small amount of information, so that the avalanche danger should be investigated especially thoroughly in the relevant locality. In some regions up to 10 cm of snow, but less in some localities, fell yesterday above approximately 2800 m.
From the afternoon the weather will be sunny at times. Some snow will fall until the early morning in some localities. Up to 2000 m rain will fall.

The new snow is bonding well with the old snowpack. Individual small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized natural avalanches are nontheless possible. As a consequence of solar radiation the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase in the afternoon.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Also high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: As a consequence of highly fluctuating temperatures the snowpack consolidated.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is moist and its surface consists of loosely bonded snow lying on a crust that is barely capable of bearing a load. In the regions exposed to snowfall this applies.

Below approximately 2500 m: The snowpack is moist. In addition, only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Slight increase in danger of moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: The danger of wet and gliding avalanches will increase during the day, reaching danger level 2 (moderate).
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to a loss of strength within the snowpack in some places in all aspects. This applies in particular on steep sunny slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: In some localities 5 to 15 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 2600 m. The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will soften during the day. Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on steep shady slopes above approximately 2700 m.
As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2700 m.
Medium-sized avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day.
Steep shady slopes above approximately 2800 m: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.
Below approximately 2200 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Above approximately 2800 m snow has fallen in some localities. Moist and wet avalanches are the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The Avalanche Warning Service currently has only a small amount of information, so that the avalanche danger should be investigated especially thoroughly in the relevant locality.
The sleet gave rise to significant moistening of the snowpack in all aspects below approximately 2800 m.
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will soften during the day. The weather will be partly cloudy.
The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase a little during the day. As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation more wet snow slides and avalanches are to be expected as the day progresses, even medium-sized ones, caution is to be exercised in particular on extremely steep north facing slopes below approximately 2900 m, and on very steep east, south and west facing slopes at high altitude.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Night: 5 cm of snow, but less in some localities, has fallen above approximately 2800 m. Up to 2800 m rain has fallen. The high humditiy will give rise to rapid settling of the snowpack over a wide area.
Thursday: Some rain will fall until late morning. Local showers will alternate with areas of clear or cloudy nights: surface refreezing of the snow could vary greatly from area to area.
In the last few days the weather was mild. This situation gave rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2900 m.
As a consequence of highly fluctuating temperatures the snowpack consolidated. This applies in particular on steep sunny slopes at high altitude, and in all aspects at intermediate altitudes.
Hardly any more avalanches were reported.
The amount of snow is subject to significant local variations.
Especially on sunny slopes below approximately 2600 m no snow is lying. On shady slopes below the tree line hardly any snow is lying.
Individual weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular in high Alpine regions.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be mostly sunny. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften quickly. Slight increase in danger of moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of new snow and wind, wind slabs formed in the last few days at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions.
As a consequence of warming there will be a gradual increase in the avalanche danger. On very steep slopes the moist avalanches can be released naturally and reach medium size in isolated cases.
Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2600 m. Avalanches can be released, mostly by large loads and reach medium size.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Below approximately 2500 m a little snow is lying. The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack. The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will soften quickly.
Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Increase in avalanche danger in the course of the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be only a slight increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Wet avalanches can in isolated cases be released by a single winter sport participant, especially on very steep sunny slopes especially above approximately 2500 m, this also applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m in gullies and bowls. In isolated cases avalanches are medium-sized.

As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of slab avalanches being released will increase gradually on very steep sunny slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions, in the regions exposed to a lot of new snow especially. Moist loose snow avalanches are to be expected as the day progresses, even medium-sized ones.

Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases and mostly by large additional loads in particular on very steep shady slopes, in particular above approximately 2500 m.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In some localities 15 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the last two days above approximately 2600 m. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good over a wide area. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

Shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

Below approximately 2000 m no snow is lying. Between approximately 2000 and 2500 m only a little snow is now lying, in particular on very steep shady slopes in gullies and bowls.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: A clear night will be followed in the early morning by favourable conditions generally. Gradual increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Increase in avalanche danger in the course of the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be only a slight increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Wet avalanches can in isolated cases be released by a single winter sport participant, especially on very steep sunny slopes especially above approximately 2500 m, this also applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m in gullies and bowls. In isolated cases avalanches are medium-sized.

As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of slab avalanches being released will increase gradually on very steep sunny slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions, in the regions exposed to a lot of new snow especially. Moist loose snow avalanches are to be expected as the day progresses, even medium-sized ones.

Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases and mostly by large additional loads in particular on very steep shady slopes, in particular above approximately 2500 m.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In some localities 15 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the last two days above approximately 2600 m. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good over a wide area. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

Shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

Below approximately 2000 m no snow is lying. Between approximately 2000 and 2500 m only a little snow is now lying, in particular on very steep shady slopes in gullies and bowls.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: A clear night will be followed in the early morning by favourable conditions generally. Gradual increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be only a slight increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Wet avalanches can in isolated cases be released by a single winter sport participant, especially on very steep sunny slopes especially above approximately 2500 m, this also applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m in gullies and bowls. In isolated cases avalanches are medium-sized.

As a consequence of solar radiation moist loose snow avalanches are to be expected as the day progresses, but they will be mostly small.

Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases and mostly by large additional loads in particular on very steep shady slopes, in particular above approximately 2500 m.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good over a wide area. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

Shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

Below approximately 2000 m no snow is lying. Between approximately 2000 and 2500 m only a little snow is now lying, in particular on very steep shady slopes in gullies and bowls.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: A clear night will be followed in the early morning by favourable conditions generally. Gradual increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be only a slight increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Wet avalanches can in isolated cases be released by a single winter sport participant, especially on very steep sunny slopes especially above approximately 2500 m, this also applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m in gullies and bowls. In isolated cases avalanches are medium-sized.

As a consequence of solar radiation moist loose snow avalanches are to be expected as the day progresses, but they will be mostly small.

Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases and mostly by large additional loads in particular on very steep shady slopes, in particular above approximately 2500 m.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good over a wide area. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

Shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

Below approximately 2000 m no snow is lying. Between approximately 2000 and 2500 m only a little snow is now lying, in particular on very steep shady slopes in gullies and bowls.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: A clear night will be followed in the early morning by favourable conditions generally. Gradual increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Only a little snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: Hardly any more wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. Avalanches are only small.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The surface of the snowpack is frozen, but not to a significant depth and will soften quickly.
Only a little snow is now lying.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Moist and wet avalanches are the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The Avalanche Warning Service currently has only a small amount of information, so that the avalanche danger should be investigated especially thoroughly in the relevant locality. Up to 2200 m rain will fall in some localities. In the early morning the weather will be cloudy.

The weather conditions gave rise to moistening of the snowpack in all aspects below approximately 2400 m.

The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night and will already be soft in the early morning. From the afternoon the weather will be sunny at times.

The new snow is bonding well with the old snowpack. Individual mostly small natural avalanches are nontheless not ruled out. As a consequence of solar radiation the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent in the afternoon.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Also above approximately 2500 m: As a consequence of mild temperatures and partly cloudy skies the snowpack consolidated during the last few days.
The surface of the snowpack cooled hardly at all during the overcast night and will soften quickly.
Below approximately 2500 m: Only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: In particular in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place more small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized gliding avalanches and wet snow slides are possible especially at high altitude.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: Below approximately 1800 m only a little snow is lying. In gullies and bowls at high altitude there is still a very large amount of snow. The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will already soften in the late morning.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will soften during the day.
In isolated cases the avalanches are small.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack consolidated. As a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already exist in the early morning. From origins in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place individual wet avalanches are possible, but they will be mostly small.
Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: Above approximately 2000 m thus far only a little snow is lying. This applies in gullies and bowls on shady slopes. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be barely evident over a wide area. The surface of the snowpack will soften quickly.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and in gullies and bowls. Individual wet and gliding avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: Snow cover is absent or patchy in many areas, up to the highest altitudes on the southern slopes; in general, the snowpack is increasingly thinning. In very isolated cases weak layers exist in the snowpack on shady slopes. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. The weather conditions will give rise to consolidation of the snowpack.
